children.content:
The logo is built on an inclined axis, which creates a sense of movement and rhythm.   The composition is assembled from elements 
of different saturation and font size, but united by a common direction and vertical center.   The upper and lower blocks visually support 
the main part, creating a balanced and dense structure.   A wide grotesque is used, emphasizing 
the technical and applied nature of the team.   The overall form maintains readability 
at different scales and is suitable for both 
digital and printed media.

children.name: vertical-spacing---all-vertical-spacing-is-defined-in-relation-to-the-height-of-the-capital-letter--b--from-the-main-logotype--the-spacing-between-the-ui-ux-tag-and-the-main-title-boiling-is-0-32b-the-spacing-between-brains-and-the-lower-tag-partners-is-0-4b
children.content:
Vertical Spacing: 
All vertical spacing is defined in relation to the height of the capital letter "B" from the main logotype.

The spacing between the UI/UX tag and the main title Boiling is 0.32B

The spacing between Brains and the lower tag Partners is 0.4B

children.name: safe-zones---the-spacing-around-the-logo-is-equal-to-the-height-of-a-capital--b--on-all-four-sides-
children.content:
Safe Zones: 
The spacing around the logo is equal to the height of a capital "B" on all four sides.

children.name: main-colors--this-color-palette-reflects-boiling-brains--approach-to-ui-ux-design--practical--calm--and-human--each-shade-was-selected-not-only-for-aesthetic-balance-but-for-its-connection-to-the-everyday-tools-and-environments-of-digital-product-work-
children.content: Main Colors  This color palette reflects Boiling Brains’ approach to UI/UX design, practical, calm, and human. Each shade was selected not only for aesthetic balance but for its connection to the everyday tools and environments of digital product work.

children.name: boiling-brains-is-a-product-design-partner-for-tech-teams-who-value-clarity---speed--and-focus--we-specialize-in-designing-functional-digital-products--web-and-mobile--with-a-calm--structured-approach-
children.content:
Boiling Brains is a product design partner for tech teams who value clarity,  speed, and focus.

We specialize in designing functional digital products, web and mobile, with a calm, structured approach.

children.name: our-position-is-between-outsource-agencies-and-internal-teams--not-freelance-hired-hands--but-not-in-house-designers-either--rather-the-best-of-both--a-flexible--reliable-design-partner-who-s-easy-to-work-with-
children.content: Our position is between outsource agencies and internal teams. Not freelance hired hands, but not in-house designers either. Rather the best of both: a flexible, reliable design partner who's easy to work with.

children.name: title-
children.content: Boiling Brains is a product design partner for tech teams who value clarity, speed, and focus

children.name: we-sound-like-a-light-flannel-shirt-looks--simple--clear--calm--no-pathos--no-unnecessary-layers--we-speak-to-be-clear--not-to-sound-smart-
children.content: We sound like a light flannel shirt looks, simple, clear, calm. No pathos, no unnecessary layers. We speak to be clear, not to sound smart.

children.name: research---validation-ux-ui-design-design-systems-audit-and-review-dev-support---qa
children.content:
research & validation
UX/UI design
design systems
audit and review
dev support & QA

Bowling Brains is a UI/UX studio run by two strong specialists. They initially reached  out to me for social media content and planning. The goal was clear: start showing  up as “studio bloggers”, publishing ideas, experience, and breakdowns on a regular basis, building a recognizable presence and gradually attracting the right audience  and inquiries. 
At the beginning of the project, we noted a simple fact: the studio didn’t yet have a brand book or a unified system that defined tone of voice, key messages, and visual rules.  So we structured the work in two stages: first, build the brand foundation, and then move into social content planning based on that foundation.

We started with a deep briefing and analysis of the studio: the team, their experience, working style, strengths, typical clients, values, and communication goals.   From there, we defined the brand direction: how the studio should sound, what it should stand for, which themes and positions support trust and expertise.   Then I created a brand book that captured the core brand foundation and communication rules, so future content would stay consistent and recognizable.

children.name: early-working-deck--defining-bowling-brains--focus-split-between-core-client-work-and-the-public-media-layer-
children.content: Early working deck: defining Bowling Brains’ focus split between core client work and the public media layer:

children.name: make-digital-products-clearer--faster--and-stronger--we-jump-in-fast--take-responsibility--and-help-teams-do-better-without-overcomplicating-things---
children.content: Make digital products clearer, faster, and stronger. We jump in fast, take responsibility, and help teams do better  without overcomplicating things.

Once the brand book was completed, we moved into content planning.   I ran a detailed discovery to understand what topics the team genuinely wanted to speak about, who they wanted to reach, and what kind of audience they wanted to build.  Social media was a long-term strategic channel for them: they planned to launch their own courses in the future, so the goal was to build consistent visibility, sharpen their voice in public, and develop a repeatable posting rhythm that could grow their follower base over time.

children.name: content-plan-dashboard--weekly-publishing-schedule-for-month-1--with-topics--post-types--ownership--status-tracking--and-visual-content-requirements-
children.content: Content plan dashboard: weekly publishing schedule for Month 1, with topics, post types, ownership, status tracking, and visual content requirements:

I built a structured monthly content planning system and produced three months of weekly post schedules.   We mapped out themes, recurring topics, and communication techniques for both LinkedIn and Instagram, with a clear rotation logic across post types: introductions, expertise, case snippets, workflow insights, and personal micro-notes.   In parallel, I supported the writing and communication side through consultations: helping shape post structure, tone of voice, and copy direction so the content stayed consistent and aligned with the brand foundation.
